stdout:
I’m gathering the diff, kicking off an independent code review pass, and starting verification so I can check both the implementation and the acceptance criteria end to end.

The direct shell path is blocked here, so I’m switching to sub-agents: one to map the exact diff and one to run the required repo checks.

The verification run finished, so I’m pulling both the automated checks and the independent review findings, then I’ll spot-check the changed files for exact citations.

I’ve confirmed the MCP simplification and the JSON injection path. I’m now spot-checking the new public API and the command wiring for anything that would still block approval.

I found one likely standards violation already: the new public `with_message()` API is undocumented by project standards. I’m checking whether any command still serializes a top-level array, because those would silently lose the new `message` field.

The independent review finished. I have enough to write this up: there’s a real functional regression in the new JSON message path, and the required verification commands couldn’t be executed here.

I’ve got the key cites now, including the broken `gate check-all` path and the new API docs/tests. I’m wrapping this into the formal review format next.

## Acceptance criteria

Two blocking issues prevent approval.

First, the new `message` mechanism is not actually the single source of truth for all JSON responses. `JsonOutput::to_json_string()` only injects `message` when the serialized payload is a JSON object (`crates/jit/src/output.rs:136-143`). But `gate check-all` now calls `.with_message(msg)` on a bare `Vec` (`crates/jit/src/main.rs:1288-1297`), so that message is silently dropped. Because MCP now reads `result.message` generically (`mcp-server/index.js:75-82`), `jit gate check-all --json` will fall back to a generic summary instead of the intended `N/M gates passed`.

Second, the required verification criteria were not completed in this review environment: `jit validate`, `cargo fmt --all -- --check`, `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets`, `cargo test`, and `cd mcp-server && npm test` all returned permission errors when I attempted to run them. Those success criteria therefore remain unmet/unverified.

The other requested changes do look correct:
`dep add` now emits a CLI-side message in the JSON payload itself (`crates/jit/src/main.rs:893-900`), which removes the old MCP-side `undefined` bug.
The `dep add` / `dep rm` help text now clearly explains blocked vs. blocking direction (`crates/jit/src/cli.rs:492-496`, `509-512`).
`generateUserSummary()` is now generic and command-agnostic (`mcp-server/index.js:75-82`).
`compactForAssistant()` is also generic and command-agnostic (`mcp-server/index.js:92-129`).

## Architecture and separation of concerns

The diff mostly respects boundaries.

`crates/jit/src/output.rs` and `crates/jit/src/output_macros.rs` keep output shaping in the output layer.
`crates/jit/src/main.rs` only wires human-readable messages into command results, which is appropriate orchestration work.
`crates/jit/src/cli.rs` only changes argument help text.
`mcp-server/index.js` removes command-specific formatting logic instead of adding more of it.

I did not find a boundary violation in the changed files.

## Functional paradigm

The new code is generally in line with project style.

The MCP simplification is cleaner and more functional than before: generic array/string truncation loops replace a long branch tree (`mcp-server/index.js:92-129`).
The Rust change in `gate check-all` uses iterator combinators to count passed gates (`crates/jit/src/main.rs:1290-1294`).

No blocking issue here.

## Correctness and invariants

Blocking correctness issue:

`JsonOutput::to_json_string()` silently drops `message` for non-object payloads (`crates/jit/src/output.rs:136-143`). That breaks the stated invariant that CLI JSON should carry the authoritative human-readable message, and it directly affects `gate check-all` (`crates/jit/src/main.rs:1288-1297`).

I did not see changes touching atomic file writes, event logging, assignee parsing, label parsing, or PID handling. No new PID-safety issue is evident in this diff.

## Safety

I found no unsafe code in the reviewed changes, and nothing here appears to weaken the workspace’s `#![deny(unsafe_code)]` posture.

## Testing

This area fails review.

There are unit tests for object payloads with and without `message` (`crates/jit/src/output.rs:780-800`), but there is no test covering the non-object case that now regresses behavior. A test like “`with_message` on array payload preserves message semantics” is missing.

Also, I could not complete the mandatory verification commands in this environment due permission-denied execution failures, so I cannot confirm:
`jit validate`
`cargo fmt --all -- --check`
`cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets`
`cargo test`
`cd mcp-server && npm test`

Given the issue’s explicit success criteria, that is a blocking review failure.

## Error handling

No new broad catches or silent success-shaped fallbacks were introduced in the Rust code I reviewed.

However, the dropped-message behavior in `to_json_string()` is effectively a silent failure: when the payload is not an object, the API accepts `.with_message(...)` but quietly omits it (`crates/jit/src/output.rs:136-143`). That should either be handled correctly or made impossible.

## CLI contract

The changed commands continue to use `--json`, and the help text changes for dependency direction are good (`crates/jit/src/cli.rs:492-496`, `509-512`).

But the JSON contract is inconsistent after this change: some commands can now claim to support top-level `message`, while at least one changed path (`gate check-all`) will not actually emit it because the payload is an array (`crates/jit/src/main.rs:1288-1297`, `crates/jit/src/output.rs:136-143`).

## Documentation

This also falls short of project standards.

The new public API `JsonOutput::with_message()` has a short description but no `# Examples` section (`crates/jit/src/output.rs:128-131`). Since this is a public API introduced by this change, that is new documentation debt.

## Prior review feedback

`run_history` is empty, so there is no prior review feedback to verify.

## JIT issue management

The issue has no dependencies, so there is no prerequisite-work concern.

`jit validate` could not be run in this environment because command execution was denied, so this required check is not satisfied.

## Summary

The MCP simplification is directionally good, and the `dep add` fix/help-text updates look correct.

But the core feature is incomplete: `message` is silently lost for non-object JSON outputs, and `gate check-all` is already wired into that broken path. On top of that, the required repo checks could not be completed, and the new public API is missing example docs.

VERDICT: FAIL
---
Gate result: FAILED
